ddls:
CREATE TABLE "interrupts" (
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt */,
  "code" TEXT /* Hexadecimal code representing the interrupt */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level assigned to the interrupt */,
  "interrupt_type" TEXT /* Type of interrupt (e.g., No interrupt, Receive data) */,
  "interrupt_source" TEXT /* Source from which the interrupt is generated */,
  "reset_control" TEXT /* Reset control mechanism for managing the interrupt */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the interrupt is currently active */,
  "last_triggered_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last time the interrupt was triggered */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the interrupt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interrupt_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source generating the interrupt */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source and its role */,
  "last_triggered_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last time the source generated an interrupt */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the source is currently active */,
  "last_reset_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last time the source was reset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority level */,
  "priority_level" TEXT /* Level of priority (e.g., 1st, 2nd, 3rd) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the priority level */,
  "default_priority" INTEGER /* Indicates if this is the default priority level */,
  "system_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the priority is system critical */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reset_controls" (
  "control_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reset control type */,
  "control_type" TEXT /* Type of control mechanism (e.g., Reading register, Writing register) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the control mechanism */,
  "last_reset_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last time the control was used */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the control is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("control_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt this configuration applies to */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source generating the interrupt */,
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* ID of the priority level assigned to the interrupt */,
  "control_id" INTEGER /* ID of the control mechanism for resetting the interrupt */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES interrupt_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_priority_id FOREIGN KEY ("priority_id") REFERENCES interrupt_priorities ("priority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_interrupt_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_id") REFERENCES interrupts ("interrupt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_configurations_control_id FOREIGN KEY ("control_id") REFERENCES reset_controls ("control_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration this version applies to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the changes in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES interrupt_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupt_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interrupt */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source generating the interrupt */,
  "triggered_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the interrupt was triggered */,
  "reset_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the interrupt was reset */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the interrupt event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_logs_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES interrupt_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupt_logs_interrupt_id FOREIGN KEY ("interrupt_id") REFERENCES interrupts ("interrupt_id")
)
